title: Delicate Flaky Slovakian Corn Dumplings
description: A traditional Slovakian dish featuring cornstarch and namul-blanch-seasoned corn, these dumplings are grassy, flaky, and perfect for a monsoon evening. This recipe is gluten-free-friendly and serves as a delicious dinner option.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine cornstarch, eggs, salt, and black pepper, and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and pliable.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape.
Place a tablespoon of namul in the center of each disk.
Fold the dough over the namul to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal.
Bring a large pot of water to a boil and gently add the dumplings.
Cook the dumplings for 10 minutes or until they float to the surface.
While the dumplings are cooking, blanch the corn kernels in boiling water for 2 minutes.
Drain the corn kernels and season with salt and black pepper.
Serve the dumplings with the seasoned corn kernels.
